description: Passphrase-KDF registry: the key-derivation functions selectable on the wire via an encryption envelope's `passphrase.alg` field, used to stretch a passphrase into a content-encryption key for the passphrase key-path (the alternative to recipient KEM slots). The pinned `params` carry the closed parameter set and security floors the structural validator enforces: a value below a floor is ENC_PASSPHRASE_ARGON2_PARAMS_TOO_LOW, an out-of-range salt is ENC_PASSPHRASE_SALT_TOO_SHORT or ENC_PASSPHRASE_SALT_TOO_LONG, an unknown `params` sub-field is SCHEMA_UNKNOWN_FIELD (the map is closed), a parameter value above 2^32 − 1 is SCHEMA_TYPE_MISMATCH, and an unregistered identifier is ENC_PASSPHRASE_ALG_UNSUPPORTED. Verifier-side upper bounds on the work factors are deployment policy, not wire rules: implementations MAY enforce non-normative, hardware-dependent ceilings against verifier-side denial of service, reporting ENC_PASSPHRASE_PARAMS_EXCEED_POLICY — a code that MUST NOT be conflated with the floor code. Note: HKDF-SHA-256 and HMAC-SHA-256 are FIXED constructions used internally (per-slot KEK derivation, the slot-set and passphrase-commitment MAC keys, content payload_key derivation, seed-to-key derivation) and carry no wire identifier — they are not selectable and are not entries here; a record carrying `passphrase.alg = "hkdf-sha256"` MUST be rejected as ENC_PASSPHRASE_ALG_UNSUPPORTED, because HKDF is designed for high-entropy inputs, not for stretching passphrases. Additions are additive-only and must ship with conformance vectors.

entries.description: Argon2id memory-hard password-hashing function, version pinned at 0x13 (19) per RFC 9106 — there is no version field on the wire and no other Argon2 version is admissible under `enc.scheme: 1`. The sole MANDATORY-to-implement passphrase KDF; v1 producers MUST emit this identifier. `params` is a closed map of exactly {m, t, p}: m (memory KiB) ≥ 65536, t (iterations) ≥ 3, p (parallelism) ≥ 1, each value a uint ≤ 2^32 − 1; the 32-byte output length is fixed and is NOT carried in `params`. The p ≥ 1 floor is a deliberate browser-compatibility constraint — security is dominated by the m × t product — and producers SHOULD use p = 4 (the second recommended profile of RFC 9106 §4) where the platform supports it. The salt MUST be 16–64 bytes inclusive and freshly drawn from a CSPRNG for every passphrase envelope.
entries.reference: RFC 9106 (Argon2 Memory-Hard Function for Password Hashing and Proof-of-Work)
